title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated rather than broad: the strongest update is a new frontier risk report from an external evaluator, alongside a notable provenance push aimed at AI-generated media transparency. The mix points to two active trust-and-safety fronts: measuring frontier-model risk with more formal reporting, and improving traceability for synthetic content in deployment.

items.title: Frontier Risk Report (February to March 2026)
items.why_it_matters: External frontier-risk reporting can shape how advanced models are evaluated, redacted, and governed.

items.title: Advancing content provenance for a safer, more transparent AI ecosystem
items.why_it_matters: Provenance tooling improves transparency and verification for AI-generated media, a key safeguard against deception.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
